mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive 20d returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which triggers negative forward S&P 500 returns as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during periods of structural labor market shifts.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer demand and fixed investment, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter as corporate revenue growth accelerates.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when one series rises while the other falls, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during an early-cycle expansion reflects improving capacity utilization and manufacturing output, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns as investors price in near-term earnings momentum.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during late-cycle regimes, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation (Rank IC < 0.01) with INDPRO growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly correlates with aggregate wage income and consumer spending power, producing positive forward S&P 500 returns as corporate earnings forecasts are revised upward.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive returns when accompanied by rising unit labor costs, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance (Rank IC < -0.01) over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates beyond a 0.4% monthly threshold, it forces expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite high inflation readings, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return periods (AUC < 0.55) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms robust aggregate demand across both production and consumption channels, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns as equity valuations expand on earnings optimism.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive power when GDP growth is driven by inventory accumulation rather than final sales, or the interaction effect vanishes (p > 0.15) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing activity and weakening economic momentum, which triggers negative short-term S&P 500 returns as recession fears dominate pricing.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when declines are isolated to specific sectors rather than broad-based, or 5-day forward returns remain positive in >50% of triggered cases.
